$GOOGL just launched Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS in public preview by bringing advanced voice control to Google AI Studio and Vertex AI.

The model supports 70+ languages, 200+ audio tags, 30 voices and SynthID watermarking for use cases across accessibility, media and enterprise.
$BIRD is in the middle of a completely absurd squeeze… up over 600%  😮

Remarkable that it took Evan over a decade to produce one profitable quarter at $SNAP.

Even more remarkable for a company that hasn't grown revenue more than 20% since 2021.

$ASML is the AI kingmaker because $TSM cannot make $NVDA most advanced GPUs without its EUV systems.

It just raised full-year guidance to $43B from $41B showing AI chip demand is still running well ahead of supply.
$BIRD is about to 4x today  💀

$IONQ is up over 30% this week after National Quantum Day and a new DARPA win tied to next-gen quantum networking.

It also comes right after $NVDA unveiled open AI models for quantum calibration which is another sign the race is shifting toward making quantum actually usable.

$BIRD is now up nearly 200% pre-market.

Selling shoes was a dead end but apparently slapping AI on the corpse is enough to bring the stock back to life.

$BIRD is selling its brand and footwear assets renaming itself NewBird AI and using a new $50M convertible facility to pivot into AI compute infrastructure by buying GPUs.

Going from selling wool sneakers to chasing AI compute might be one of the wildest pivots this cycle.

Jane Street invested another $1B in $CRWV at $109 per share and also plans to spend ~$6B on CoreWeave’s tech offerings.

It marks CoreWeave’s third multibillion-dollar deal this month following major commitments from $META and Anthropic.

$SNAP spent a historic bull market proving that user growth without real monetization leverage doesn’t matter.

Being down over 90% in an environment like this is pretty hard to do.
US AND IRAN AGREE IN PRINCIPLE TO EXTEND THE TRUCE

$SNAP is laying off ~16% of its workforce while guiding for ~$1.53B in Q1 revenue, ~$233M EBITDA and up to $130M in restructuring charges.

Tough scene for morale when layoffs hit right after the CEO Evan Spiegel is having the time of his life at Coachella.

$TSLA just taped out AI5 which is a major chip milestone that could materially boost FSD, Robotaxi & Optimus with a big step up in edge-AI compute versus AI4.

$TSM helped bring AI5 to production while Tesla says AI6, Dojo 3 & other chips are already in the works.

New taxes on everyday expenses like dry cleaning, gym memberships & pet care.

Dodge County Sheriff Dale Schmidt filed a $1M federal defamation lawsuit against Naqvi & Cook County Commissioner Kevin Morrison, supported by hotel records showing she stayed at a Hampton Inn with spa access, gym use, and texts about expenses.

First full day of the U.S. naval blockade on Iran:
- Zero vessels left Iranian ports
- Six merchant ships turned back after American warnings
- No shots fired, no enforcement needed

Meanwhile, traffic through the Strait of Hormuz continued normally with over 20 vessels passing through.

The blockade is off to a quiet but effective start.

Iran blinked first, ships are turning around without a fight.

The real test will come in the next few days when Tehran decides whether to push back or keep playing it safe.

Source: WSJ

Bloom expanded its agreement with Oracle to provide up to 2.8GW of fuel cells for Oracle’s data centers, with 1.2GW initially contracted.

This deal “underscores Bloom’s capability to provide fast, reliable power suited for AI workloads, which require rapid, load‑following support that traditional grids were not designed to deliver.”

$BE $ORCL

CoreWeave also announced a multi-year agreement with Anthropic to help the AI firm run workloads at production scale, starting with a phased infrastructure roll-out with potential to expand over time.

$CRWV $NVDA

TSMC reported March revenue of NT$415.9 billion, up 45.2% YoY and 30.7% MoM on strong AI demand. Q1 revenue was NT$1.13 trillion, up 35.1% YoY and in line with estimates for NT$1.12 trillion.

$TSM $NVDA $AMD $AVGO

CoreWeave recently expanded its long-term agreement with Meta, providing the social media giant with AI cloud capacity through 2032 for $21 billion, including some initial deployments of Nvidia’s Vera Rubin platform. 

$CRWV $META $NVDA

Google recently expanded its chip partnership with Intel, committing to using multiple generations of Intel’s CPUs in its AI data centers, and continuing development of ASIC-based infrastructure processing units (IPUs) that offload networking, storage and security functions from host CPUs.

📺 OVERBOUGHT MARKET… BUT STILL HIGHER?

We had a massive V-shaped rally from the bottom, going from ~$629.29 to ~$694.50 in $SPY. This kind of move typically catches people off guard and leaves many underexposed.

As I mentioned previously, the real turning point came around April 2nd, when the market held up despite rising #crudeoil, sellers lost control, and a higher low formed, confirming strength.

Then key technical triggers: an inverted head-and-shoulders pattern, gap-ups with no easy entry, and a break above the ~$671.50 level, which led to a strong continuation.

This was a high-momentum, institutionally driven move, not a weak bounce as everyone initially thought.

*

This rally wasn’t narrow — it expanded across various sectors:
– Early leaders like memory stocks ($SNDK $WDC $STX and even $MU) and semiconductors ($DELL $MRVL);
– Then rotation into Mag 7 names $GOOGL $AMZN even $META → “repair rally”;
– Then further down the food chain: smaller names $IREN $CIFR $NBIS, even Bitcoin-related names like $HOOD.

This is important: when leadership broadens like this, it usually confirms a healthy uptrend, not a dead-cat bounce.

*

However, the market is now extended: oscillator at +75, which means overbought. This is now Day 8 of the move. It’s hard to buy up here.

I even started hedging, though it cost money (the trend is too strong).

Ideally, we want markets to go sideways, let moving averages catch up, momentum to cool off, and new setups to form.

After a move this strong, continuation is more likely than collapse, but it needs a pause.

*

So, we want this today:

– $SPY around ~$693. Ideally, we want a small pullback or sideways action, then continue higher.
– $QQQ huge move back into resistance. Ideally, we want it to hold here and form a bull flag.

*

So, this is a powerful, broad-based V-shaped rally. The market is overbought short-term, but bullish structurally. 

Most likely path: pause / sideways consolidation, then higher highs in the coming weeks/months.

Don't fight the trend or wait for a perfect pullback that may never come. Stay involved, manage risk, and be ready to act quickly.

Gold also rebounding after the temporary liquidation of Gold from Gulf Nations that needed liquidity to cover obligations.  Other nations say "Thank you" for the cheaper prices.  

Print Fiat.  Stack Bars.  The Central Bank octane.

$GLD near $4,800/oz — gold is rallying alongside equities. Risk-on AND safe-haven demand surging simultaneously. That's not normal. Dollar weakness plus geopolitical uncertainty is the fuel. The gold paradox is flashing a macro signal.

$USO $WTI crude dropped from $104 to $93 on Apr 14 as peace talks advanced and the IEA slashed its oil demand outlook — expecting an 80kb/d contraction this year. War premium evaporating in real time.

$JPM just posted $11.6B in trading revenue — a record, up 20% YoY. Net income: $16.5B. EPS of $5.94 beat estimates by 9%. Yet Dimon called risks 'increasingly complex.' The stock slipped pre-market. Markets heard the warning.
$QQQ just logged its 10th straight daily gain — the Nasdaq's longest winning streak since Dec 2023. Up 12.5%+ from the March 30 low. Tech is leading this rally, and the momentum is unmistakable.
